pattern: Greedy reach-extension: track the largest prefix [1, reach] you can already guarantee, and every time the next coin can't extend it, plug the smallest possible gap value yourself before moving on.
hints: Sort the coins first, and think in terms of a running frontier: the largest value R such that every integer from 1 to R is already obtainable from coins processed so far.

If the next available coin is worth more than R + 1, no coin you own can extend the frontier past R, so the cheapest fix is to imagine adding a coin worth exactly R + 1, which doubles-plus-one your frontier in one move.
Otherwise fold the next coin into the frontier (R += coin) and advance past it; repeat until the frontier reaches or passes target, counting only the imagined coins you had to invent.

edge_inputs.description: single coin already equal to target
single coin that skips over the only needed value
many unit coins already covering the whole target range
one coin far larger than the frontier forces several invented coins before it can be used
a huge coin that is never usable because it exceeds every frontier value reached
target at its minimum with a coin far above it
sparse single small coin against a much larger target, forcing a chain of invented coins
duplicate coins with a gap in the middle of the achievable range

stress.description: large random coin pool near maximum size and target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    target = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    coins = [rng.randint(1, target) for _ in range(size)]
    return [coins, target]

stress.description: many oversized coins that never contribute, exercising the pure-scan performance path
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    target = 100000
    coins = [rng.randint(target // 2, target) for _ in range(size)]
    return [coins, target]
